question.text: To qualify as an Angel Investor for investing in an Angel Fund (sub-category of Cat I AIF), an individual must have net tangible assets of at least:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An Angel Investor must have net tangible assets of at least Rs. 2 crore (excluding the value of principal residence) AND have early-stage investment experience, serial entrepreneurship experience, or 10+ years of senior management experience. **The Trap:** The Rs. 2 crore specifically excludes the principal residence — so a person with only a Rs. 3 crore house and no other assets doesn't qualify. **Cross-Exam Context:** Angel fund/investor criteria are tested in X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Angel investing is the highest-risk stage of startup investing — ensure clients understand that most angel investments may result in total loss, and only truly surplus capital should be deployed.
